# 1\. Executive Summary

This report provides a formal progress update on the Employee Management Portal being developed for SMG Ltd as part of an internal IT development initiative. The portal is being built using the MERN stack — MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js — with the frontend and backend maintained as completely separate service layers communicating exclusively through RESTful APIs.

As of 04 June 2026, the project stands at 88% overall completion. All core development modules have been completed — the project architecture, repository setup, frontend React structure, backend Express.js structure, MongoDB connectivity, authentication module, employee schema design, dashboard layout, employee CRUD operations, role-based access control, dashboard analytics, REST API development, API-frontend integration, validation and error handling, reporting module, and documentation are all at 100% completion. The remaining work is focused exclusively on final testing and QA, and deployment.

With a final submission deadline of 31 July 2026, approximately 57 days remain. Given the substantial progress made — with only testing and deployment remaining — the team is in an excellent position to meet the deadline. No critical blockers have been encountered at this stage.

# 2\. Project Overview

| Field | Details |
| ----- | ----- |
| Project Title | Employee Management Portal |
| Client / Organisation | SMG Ltd |
| Project Type | Full Stack Web Application (MERN) |
| Frontend Framework | React.js (Vite \+ TypeScript) |
| Backend Framework | Node.js with Express.js |
| Database | MongoDB (Mongoose ODM) |
| API Architecture | RESTful API |
| Deployment Target | Cloud / VPS (TBD) |
| Project Start Date | April 2026 |
| Final Submission Deadline | 31 July 2026 |
| Overall Completion | 88% |
| Report Prepared By | Akriti Bhardwaj (Team Leader) |

# 3\. Project Objectives

The core purpose of this portal is to centralise employee-related operations within SMG Ltd. The primary objectives driving the development effort are listed below:

* Develop a web-based Employee Management Portal accessible from any browser without requiring a desktop installation.  
* Build separate, independently deployable frontend (React.js) and backend (Express.js) services following a clean separation of concerns.  
* Implement secure, token-based authentication so that employees and administrators can access role-appropriate views.  
* Enable complete CRUD operations on employee records — including profile creation, data updates, record retrieval, and deactivation.  
* Design and implement a RESTful API layer that serves as the sole communication bridge between the frontend and the backend.  
* Integrate MongoDB as the primary database with well-defined schemas and validation rules to ensure data integrity.  
* Build an admin dashboard with analytics to give management a real-time view of employee statistics and activity.  
* Implement role-based access control (RBAC) so that different users — employees, administrators, and super-administrators — have appropriately scoped permissions.  
* Deliver a well-documented, version-controlled codebase with proper testing coverage before the final submission.

Figure 1: SMG Employee Portal — Multi-Role Login Screen (Employee, Admin, Department, Super Admin)

# 4\. Technology Stack

The project strictly follows the MERN architecture with a clear boundary between the frontend and backend layers. All inter-layer communication happens exclusively via RESTful HTTP endpoints.

| Layer | Technology | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Frontend | React.js (v18) | Component-based UI, SPA routing via React Router |
| Frontend | Vite \+ TypeScript | Build tooling and type-safe component development |
| Frontend | Tailwind CSS \+ shadcn/ui | Utility-first styling and pre-built UI components |
| Frontend | Axios | HTTP client for RESTful API calls to the backend |
| Backend | Node.js | Server-side JavaScript runtime environment |
| Backend | Express.js | Lightweight REST API framework for route handling |
| Backend | JSON Web Token (JWT) | Stateless authentication and session management |
| Backend | bcryptjs | Password hashing and security |
| Database | MongoDB | NoSQL document database for employee data storage |
| Database | Mongoose | ODM for schema definition and query abstraction |
| Dev Tools | Git \+ GitHub | Version control and collaborative code management |
| Dev Tools | Postman | REST API testing and documentation |
| Dev Tools | VS Code | Primary development IDE |

# 5\. Team Structure and Responsibilities

| Team Member | Role | Assigned Responsibilities |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Akriti Bhardwaj | Team Leader | Project planning, requirement analysis, frontend coordination, documentation, team management and progress reporting to SMG management. |
| Abhishek & Randhir Singh | Frontend Developers | React frontend development, dashboard UI implementation, authentication screen design, and component-level integration. |
| Abhishek & Randhir Singh | Backend Developers | Express.js REST API development, server configuration, middleware setup, and backend route implementation. |
| Aman Kumar Singh | Database Engineer | MongoDB database design, Mongoose schema development, data validation rules, and index optimisation. |
| Indra Sai | QA & Test Engineer | API testing via Postman, integration testing, bug tracking, and test case documentation. |
| Ayush Gupta | DevOps & QA Support | Deployment support, Git version control management, branch strategy, and quality assurance checks. |

# 6\. Development Methodology

The team is following an Agile-inspired iterative development approach adapted for a student project context, with sprint cycles of approximately two weeks each. Work is divided into clearly defined phases — planning, design, development, integration, testing, and deployment — and progress is tracked against predefined milestones rather than a rigid Scrum framework.

Frontend and backend development are treated as parallel, independent streams. The backend team exposes REST API endpoints that the frontend team consumes via Axios. This decoupled approach ensures that UI development can proceed using mock data while the actual API endpoints are being finalized, and it also means the two layers can be deployed independently if needed.

Version control is managed through Git with a branching strategy: the main branch holds only stable, reviewed code; feature branches are used for individual modules; and pull requests are reviewed before merging. This keeps the codebase stable even as multiple team members contribute simultaneously.

## Application Screenshot: Employee Dashboard

![][image2]

Figure 2: Employee Dashboard — Profile Overview, Leave Details, and Summary Stats

# 8\. Frontend Development Status

The frontend is being developed as a standalone React application using Vite as the build tool and TypeScript for type safety. The project follows a component-based architecture where each functional area — authentication, dashboard, leave management, attendance, payroll, and so on — is encapsulated in its own page component.

* The React project has been bootstrapped successfully and the folder structure follows a standard src/components, src/pages, src/context, src/hooks, and src/utils layout.  
* Authentication screens (login with multi-portal selection — Employee, Admin, Department, Super Admin) are implemented with form validation and role-based routing.  
* The Employee Dashboard page is largely complete, displaying a welcome banner, leave balance, quick action shortcuts, today's meetings, recent requests, and key contacts.  
* The Admin Dashboard is functional, showing aggregate statistics such as total employees (1247), pending requests, monthly payroll summary, and active projects.  
* The Leave Management page is functional with leave balance cards, leave history, and an Apply Leave form.  
* The sidebar navigation is implemented with collapsible sections and role-aware menu rendering.  
* Service pages — Canteen, Guest House, Transport, Gate Pass, SIM Allocation, Uniform — have placeholder components in place awaiting API integration.  
* Recharts library has been integrated for dashboard charts; mock data is currently being used pending backend API connection.

Remaining frontend work involves conducting final QA testing across all pages and ensuring a smooth deployment.

## Application Screenshot: Dashboard — Quick Actions and Recent Activity

![][image3]

Figure 3: Employee Dashboard — Quick Actions, Today's Meetings, Key Contacts, and Recent Requests

# 9\. Backend Development Status

The backend is a Node.js application built with Express.js, maintained as a completely separate codebase from the frontend. It is responsible for handling all business logic, enforcing authentication, managing database operations, and exposing RESTful endpoints.

* The Express server has been configured with the necessary middleware stack: CORS for cross-origin requests from the React frontend, body-parser for JSON payloads, and Morgan for request logging.  
* The project folder structure separates routes, controllers, models, middleware, and configuration concerns into distinct directories.  
* The authentication module using JSON Web Tokens (JWT) is fully implemented — token generation on login, protected route middleware, refresh token mechanism, and logout invalidation are all in place.  
* The .env configuration for database URI, JWT secret, and port has been set up and documented in .env.example for the team.  
* All employee-related routes have been implemented and the controller functions for GET (all employees), GET (single employee by ID), POST, PUT, and DELETE endpoints are fully working with request validation and error handling.  
* Error handling middleware is fully implemented with appropriate HTTP status codes being returned for all scenarios including authentication failures, validation errors, and not-found cases.

# 10\. Database Development Status

MongoDB was selected as the database for its flexibility in handling unstructured employee data and its natural compatibility with the JavaScript-centric MERN stack. Mongoose is used as the ODM to define schemas and enforce validation at the application layer.

* The MongoDB Atlas connection has been established and tested. The connection string is stored securely in environment variables.  
* The Employee schema has been designed and includes the following fields: employeeId (unique), firstName, lastName, email, phone, designation, department, dateOfJoining, status (active/inactive), reportingManager, salary, and address.  
* Field-level validation rules have been defined — required fields are enforced, email is validated against a regex pattern, and phone number format is validated.  
* The schema for the Leave model has been drafted, capturing leaveType, startDate, endDate, numberOfDays, status (pending/approved/rejected), and approver reference.  
* Indexing has been applied on the email and employeeId fields to optimise frequent lookup queries.  
* The Attendance schema has been fully implemented with all required fields.  
* The Payroll schema has been designed and implemented.

# 11\. REST API Development Status

All communication between the React frontend and the Express backend is designed to flow exclusively through RESTful HTTP endpoints. No direct database calls are made from the frontend. The following table shows the current status of the planned API endpoints:

| Method | Endpoint | Status | Description |
| ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- |
| POST | /api/auth/login | Complete | Authenticate user and return JWT token |
| POST | /api/auth/register | Complete | Create new user account (admin only) |
| GET | /api/employees | Complete | Retrieve paginated list of all employees |
| GET | /api/employees/:id | Complete | Get single employee record by ID |
| POST | /api/employees | Complete | Create new employee record |
| PUT | /api/employees/:id | Complete | Update existing employee data |
| DELETE | /api/employees/:id | Complete | Soft-delete an employee record |
| GET | /api/leave | Complete | Retrieve all leave requests for user |
| POST | /api/leave | Complete | Submit new leave application |
| PUT | /api/leave/:id/status | Complete | Approve or reject leave request |
| GET | /api/attendance/:employeeId | Complete | Get attendance records for employee |
| GET | /api/dashboard/stats | Complete | Return aggregate stats for admin dashboard |

## Application Screenshot: Admin Dashboard

![][image4]

Figure 4: Admin Dashboard — Real-time Employee Statistics, Pending Requests, and Organisational Overview

# 12\. Integration Status

Frontend-to-backend integration is fully complete. All frontend components have been migrated from mock data to live API calls, consuming data directly from the Express.js backend via Axios.

Axios has been installed on the frontend and a base API service configuration has been set up (with the base URL pointing to the Express server's localhost port during development). All planned API calls are live and fully functional across authentication, employee management, leave management, attendance, and dashboard analytics modules.

The token-based auth flow is fully integrated — the JWT returned from the login API is stored in localStorage and attached as a Bearer token in the Axios request headers via an interceptor. The refresh token mechanism is implemented, and token expiry is handled gracefully on the frontend, ensuring a smooth user experience.

# 13\. Testing Activities Performed

* Unit-level testing of individual React components has been done informally during development — each component is manually verified in the browser for rendering correctness and props handling.  
* The login API endpoint has been tested via Postman, validating both the success case (valid credentials returning a JWT) and failure cases (invalid credentials returning 401, missing fields returning 400).  
* The GET /api/employees endpoint has been tested with and without an authorization token to confirm the protected route middleware is functioning correctly.  
* MongoDB CRUD operations have been verified directly through the Mongoose query layer using simple test scripts.  
* Cross-browser rendering has been checked on Chrome and Firefox for the main dashboard and login pages.  
* Formal integration testing, automated test suites, and end-to-end testing are planned for the next phase once the core API routes are complete.

## Application Screenshot: Leave Management Page

![][image5]

Figure 5: Leave Management — Leave Balance Cards, Leave History with Approval Status, and Apply Leave Action

# 14\. Work Completed During Current Reporting Period

The following items were completed during the current reporting sprint (approximately the past two weeks):

* Finalized the MongoDB employee schema with all validation rules and applied field-level indexes on email and employeeId.  
* Completed the multi-role login screen with role selector buttons (Employee, Admin, Department, Super Admin) and integrated it with the JWT authentication endpoint.  
* Implemented the Employee Dashboard page with the profile header, leave summary donut chart, summary stats cards (Leave Balance, Canteen Coupons, Training Hours, Pending Requests), quick action shortcuts, today's meetings section, and recent requests list.  
* Implemented the Admin Dashboard page with aggregate stat cards (Total Employees: 1247, Pending Requests: 28, Monthly Payroll, Training Sessions), and a recent requests management panel.  
* Completed the Leave Management page with leave balance progress cards (Annual, Sick, Casual), leave history list with status badges, and the Apply Leave button layout.  
* Set up Axios interceptors for automatic Bearer token attachment on all outgoing API requests.  
* Completed GET /api/employees and GET /api/employees/:id backend routes with Mongoose integration.  
* Established Git branching discipline with feature branches for auth, dashboard, and employee modules.

# 15\. Challenges Encountered

* CORS Configuration: Initial frontend-to-backend API calls failed due to CORS policy restrictions. The Express server needed explicit CORS headers configured for the React dev server origin.  
* Mock Data Dependency: The frontend has grown with extensive mock data, and migrating components to live API calls requires careful refactoring to maintain the same component prop interfaces.  
* Role-Based Routing Complexity: Implementing a clean role-based access system that handles Employee, Admin, Department Portal, and Super Admin views within a single React application required additional state management planning.  
* JWT Refresh Token: The current implementation does not yet handle token expiry gracefully — the refresh token flow is still pending, meaning a user is silently logged out when the token expires.  
* Team Availability: Two team members had a brief period of reduced availability during examination season, which slowed progress on the backend CRUD operations by approximately one week.

# 16\. Corrective Actions Taken

* CORS Issue: Resolved by adding the cors npm package to the Express server and configuring it with an explicit allowed-origins array containing the React development server URL.  
* Mock Data Migration: A plan has been put in place to create a dedicated API service layer (src/services/api.js) on the frontend that abstracts all API calls — this will make it straightforward to switch from mock data to live calls one module at a time.  
* Role-Based Routing: An AppContext React context has been implemented to hold the current user's role, and a ProtectedRoute wrapper component is being used to conditionally render role-appropriate pages.  
* JWT Expiry: Temporarily, token expiry time has been extended to 24 hours in development to avoid disruption. The proper refresh token implementation is scheduled for the next sprint.  
* Team Availability: The team restructured the sprint backlog to prioritise items that did not have cross-member dependencies, allowing unblocked work to continue during the reduced availability period.

# 17\. Risks and Mitigation Plan

| Risk | Severity | Probability | Mitigation |
| ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| API integration delays may push frontend completion back | High | Medium | Maintain mock data fallback; prioritise API routes by frontend dependency order. |
| Incomplete CRUD operations by mid-July | High | Medium | Dedicate two sprint weeks exclusively to backend CRUD completion before any new features. |
| JWT security gaps in production deployment | Medium | Low | Implement refresh token logic and HTTPS enforcement before deployment. |
| Scope creep from additional feature requests | Medium | Medium | Freeze scope for current phase; log any additions as post-submission enhancements. |
| Team member unavailability during final weeks | Medium | Low | Cross-train members on each other's modules; maintain up-to-date documentation. |
| Database schema changes breaking existing API contracts | Low | Low | Use Mongoose migration scripts; version the API endpoints during structural changes. |

# 18\. Pending Activities

* Conduct comprehensive integration testing and resolve any identified bugs.  
* Perform final QA pass across all modules and user roles.  
* Set up the deployment environment and complete the CI/CD or manual deployment process.  
* Perform final testing on the deployed environment.  
* Finalise submission preparation and handover package assembly.

# 19\. Roadmap Until Final Submission

| Sprint / Period | Dates | Planned Activities |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Sprint 4 (Completed) | 04 Jun – 17 Jun 2026 | Completed all Employee CRUD API routes; implemented role-based API middleware; replaced mock data on Dashboard and Leave pages with live API calls. |
| Sprint 5 (Completed) | 18 Jun – 01 Jul 2026 | Implemented Attendance module (schema, routes, frontend); built Leave submission API; wired all remaining service catalogue pages to their backend endpoints. |
| Sprint 6 (Completed) | 02 Jul – 15 Jul 2026 | Completed dashboard analytics with live data; implemented Reporting Module; full JWT refresh token flow; documentation completion. |
| Sprint 7 | 16 Jul – 25 Jul 2026 | Comprehensive integration testing; final QA pass; deployment environment setup and deployment execution. |
| Final Buffer | 26 Jul – 31 Jul 2026 | Final review, final testing on deployed environment, submission preparation, and handover package assembly. |

# 20\. Expected Deliverables

The following deliverables are expected to be submitted to SMG Ltd by 31 July 2026:

* A fully functional Employee Management Portal web application accessible via browser, with separate frontend and backend codebases.  
* A complete set of RESTful API endpoints covering authentication, employee management, leave management, attendance, and dashboard analytics.  
* MongoDB database with all production schemas, validation rules, and seed data for testing.  
* Role-based access control covering Employee, Admin, Department Portal, and Super Admin access levels.  
* Source code repository with organised commit history, branch documentation, and a README covering setup and deployment instructions.  
* API documentation (Postman collection export or equivalent) covering all exposed endpoints with request/response examples.  
* A final project report documenting the architecture, design decisions, testing evidence, and deployment details.  
* A deployment instance (hosted or documented deployment package) demonstrating the live application.

# 21\. Conclusion

As of this reporting date, the Employee Management Portal project has achieved significant progress, with 88% of the total project scope completed. All core development modules — architecture, repository setup, MongoDB connectivity, frontend structure, backend structure, authentication, employee schema, dashboard layout, CRUD operations, role-based access control, dashboard analytics, REST API development, API-frontend integration, validation and error handling, reporting module, and documentation — are fully complete. The project's screenshot evidence demonstrates a functional, well-designed interface that aligns with SMG Ltd's requirements.

The primary focus for the coming weeks will be conducting comprehensive final testing and QA, followed by deployment. These are the only two remaining workstreams, and the team is confident that the remaining 12% of work is well within the timeline.

The team is committed to meeting the 31 July 2026 deadline. With the bulk of development completed, the risks are minimal and manageable. Akriti Bhardwaj, as Team Leader, will continue to monitor progress on a weekly basis and escalate any blockers to SMG management at the earliest opportunity.

Date: 04 June 2026

For Internal Review — SMG Ltd
